title: CircleCI Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for CircleCI CLI commands and configuration

commands.description: Login to CircleCI from the command line
commands.scenario: Login to CircleCI from the command line

commands.description: Run a job locally using CircleCI CLI
commands.scenario: Run a job locally using CircleCI CLI

commands.description: Define a version for CircleCI configuration
commands.scenario: Define a version for CircleCI configuration

commands.description: Specify a Docker executor in CircleCI config
commands.scenario: Specify a Docker executor in CircleCI config

commands.command:
jobs:
  build:
    docker:
      - image: circleci/node:14
    steps:
      - checkout
      - run: npm install
commands.description: Define a simple job in CircleCI
commands.scenario: Define a simple job in CircleCI

commands.command:
steps:
  - restore_cache:
      key: dependency-cache-{{ checksum "package-lock.json" }}
  - run: npm install
  - save_cache:
      key: dependency-cache-{{ checksum "package-lock.json" }}
      paths:
        - node_modules
commands.description: Use caching to speed up builds
commands.scenario: Use caching to speed up builds

commands.command:
workflows:
  version: 2
  build_and_test:
    jobs:
      - build
    triggers:
      - schedule:
          cron: "0 0 * * 0"
          filters:
            branches:
              only:
                - main

jobs:
  build:
    docker:
      - image: circleci/node:14
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
    steps:
      - checkout
      - run: npm install

commands.description: Retrieve usage statistics and consumption data via the CircleCI API to monitor build minutes and resource usage.
Define restricted input values for reusable commands or jobs to ensure strict configuration validation.
Inject specific environment variables into a local execution to simulate different deployment or testing contexts.
Implement conditional step execution within a job based on parameter values or logic.
Assign specific hardware profiles (e.g., medium, large, xlarge) to a job to optimize performance for CPU/RAM intensive tasks.
